label: United States Population Density Validation Map
subject: Human population density
units: Raw density in persons per square kilometer, plus log1p and normalized debug fields
description: Validation-first U.S. population density dataset built from the WorldPop 2020 1 km density image service, resampled into a projected display grid without smoothing.

sourceMetadata.nominalResolutionLabel: 30 arc-second geographic raster marketed as 1 km population density
sourceMetadata.valueMeaning: Each source pixel is a single-band floating-point population density estimate in persons per square kilometer.
sourceMetadata.serviceDescription: This service provides access to a cloud-optimized lossless reformatting of WorldPop's 1-km annual estimates of population density from the year 2000 to 2020. Calculated from the WorldPop 1km Total Population 2000-2020. The DOI for the original datasets is 10.5258/SOTON/WP00674. Recommended Citation: WorldPop (www.worldpop.org - School of Geography and Environmental Science, University of Southampton; Department of Geography and Geosciences, University of Louisville; Departement de Geographie, Universite de Namur) and Center for International Earth Science Information Network (CIESIN), Columbia University (2018). Global High Resolution Population Denominators Project - Funded by The Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation. Accessed from https://worldpop.arcgis.com/arcgis/rest/services/WorldPop_Total_Population_1km/ImageServer, which was acquired from https://www.worldpop.org/doi/10.5258/SOTON/WP00674 on 15 Sep, 2021.

sources.label: WorldPop Population Density 1 km ImageServer metadata
WorldPop export window used for preprocessing

validationNotes: Top sampled metro source values are New York City (23916 /km²), Miami (9916 /km²), Los Angeles (5906 /km²), Bay Area (3761 /km²).
Southern California's regional average density (1181 /km²) is above the Northeast corridor (647 /km²), so that contrast should be reviewed visually in phase 2.
Great Lakes / Chicago, Texas triangle, and Florida peninsula all register strong regional signals with mean densities of 200, 219, and 486 /km² respectively.
The single highest projected display cell is centered near 40.5988°, -73.7045° with raw density 14131 /km².
